text:
It is an allied medical university.
In the past, some who did not meet the medical cutoff marks chose this university.
How many years does it take?
It takes a total of 4 years, including a research thesis.
What do you learn?
Anatomy, Physiology, Pharmacology, Pathology + Preventive and Social Medicine (Epidemiology, Communicable Diseases, Behavioural Science, Health Education, Environmental Sanitation, Community Health, Occupational Health, Statistics) + Bedside clinicals (Medicine, Surgeon) + Research thesis.
After completing these, you will receive a Bachelor of Community Health (B.Comm.H) medical science degree.
When entering the workforce, where and in what position will you work?
Unlike doctors who can join as commanding officers, you join at a higher-level position, so it is not considered a low rank.
You will work as a Health Assistant at the Naypyidaw head office, Region/District/Township Public Health Departments, and Rural Health Centers under the Department of Public Health.
You can also work as a university teacher.
Job opportunities are much more abundant than for doctors, medical technologists, and pharmacists.
In small townships, only 3-6 doctors and 1-2 pharmacists/medical technologists are appointed, but up to 5-13 Health Assistants can be appointed.
This is just at the township level.
If you work at a Rural Health Center (RHC), the highest position is the Health Assistant.
Under them are Female Health Workers (nurses in blue), Midwives (nurses in red), and Public Health Supervisor Grade IIs.
Being the highest rank at the RHC, you supervise 4-8 sub-centers and 11-19 staff members.
They are like the administrators of a township's rural areas.

Under the Township Medical Officer and Township Health Assistant Grade 1, each Health Assistant manages the public health sector of their assigned area.
They are the first to provide healthcare to a population of 9,000-15,000 across 50-100 villages, which is why it is called the University of Community Health.
There are 3 types of Health Assistants: RHA, CHA, and B.Comm.H HA.
Since B.Comm.H HAs hold a medical science degree, they can attend the Master of Public Health (MPH) program, which is open to doctors, and pursue a PhD alongside doctors.
What do you do in the workplace?
The main role is Leadership & Management.
You lead and supervise the systematic implementation of every health project under the Department of Public Health by your staff.
What tasks do you supervise?
Overseeing clinic operations, field healthcare, maternal and reproductive health, child health development, vaccination, infectious disease surveillance, non-communicable disease prevention and treatment, injuries, vital statistics collection, school health, environmental sanitation, health education, disaster management, proper usage of medical supplies, and attending training sessions.
For those who do not enter government service, this degree is highly favored by domestic NGOs and INGOs, though a specified compensation bond must be paid.
Career advancement goes up to Health Assistant, Health Assistant Grade 1, Township Health Assistant / Officer, and Assistant Director.
Q: Is the University of Community Health only in Magway? A: Yes, it is only in Magway.
Q: How much do you earn per month if you become a Health Assistant? A: Currently, it is over 310,000 kyats.

Q: I want to know about RHA and CHA. What are they? A: Rural Health Assistant (RHA) and Community Health Assistant (CHA). These are both Health Assistants; the terms and duties differ based on location. For example, an RHA serves in a rural area and performs basic treatment and clinical care at a rural health center. A CHA is involved in both rural and urban areas; their main duty is to prevent diseases and promote community health. They conduct health education, mobilization, disease prevention programs, and data collection. RHA = Rural clinic-based work (Treatment + Basic care). CHA = Community-based work (Prevention + Education/Mobilization).
Q: Can I work as an RHA after attending the University of Community Health? A: No, you probably cannot.

Q: If I fail other admissions, can I transfer to UCH? I want to transfer if I don't get into Pharmacy with 425 marks. If I don't get into the University of Education, can I transfer to UCH? A: It should be possible if the specified student quota isn't full, sis.

Q: If I want to transfer to UCH, should I inquire directly at the university or go to the DHE (Department of Higher Education)? A: 09 774 812108 - Tun Hla Aung from Student Affairs. 09 256 144 386 - If the first number doesn't work, try calling this one. I hope everything goes smoothly.

Q: I applied as a boy with 359 marks. Is there a chance I'll get in? I really want to attend. A: Last year, the minimum for boys was 320 and for girls, it was 420. You should be able to get in, bro. The quota for boys is 80%, bro.
Q: When will the admission results be known? A: They said around next week.
Q: I didn't include CH in the application form. If a major is assigned, can I still change it? A: A senior sister said it's not possible to do that.
Q: Can I transfer from Basic Health back to Maubin Agriculture if my marks meet the requirement? A: Yes, it is included.

Q: Does anyone know the phone numbers for UCH Student Affairs? A: 09 774 812108 - Tun Hla Aung from Student Affairs. 09 256 144 386 - If the first number doesn't work, try calling this one. I hope everything goes smoothly.
Q: What were the ECB cutoff marks last year? A: Last year's cutoff for the three ECB subjects combined was 238 for girls and 203 for boys. For 2023, it was 261 for girls and 248 for boys.
Q: How many boys and girls are accepted? A: For Dental, it's 60% boys and 40% girls, so there are more chances for boys.

Now that the matriculation exam results are out, for brothers and sisters choosing a university, we'd like to introduce the career prospects, subjects taught, scope of the field, and interesting facts about our University of Dental Medicine.
There are only two dental universities: UDM (Yangon) and UDM (Mandalay).
Yangon UDM is on Thanthuma Road, Thingangyun Township.
If you take the bus, drop off at Chan Aye bus stop.
Under the new system, it takes a total of 5 years: Foundation year (2 months), Year 1, 2, 3, Final year, and House Surgeon to get the Bachelor of Dental Surgery (B.D.S) degree.
For us, it was 6 years.
You'll study medical subjects like Anatomy, Physiology, Biochemistry, General Pathology, Microbiology, Pharmacology, General Medicine, and General Surgery.
In Foundation year (2 months) you study Myan, Eng, Math, Phy, Chem.
Year 1 (second yr): Anatomy, Physio, Biochem, Oral biological science.
Stethoscopes are used in physio practicals.
Year 2 (third yr): Pathology, Pharmacology, Microbiology, Prosthetic, Conservative dentistry.
For conservative, there is JOC (Junior Operative Course) which must be passed before handling patients.
Year 3 (fourth yr): General Medicine, General Surgery, Clinical Dentistry I (Oral Surgery and Oral Medicine), Clinical Dentistry II (Periodontology, Preventive and Community Dentistry, Preventive and Social Medicine), Clinical Dentistry III (Paediatric Dentistry & Orthodontics), Senior operative dentistry (Conservative dentistry), Prosthodontics.
Fourth year includes hospital visits and field trips.
Final year covers Oral and Maxillofacial surgery, Oral Medicine, Conservative Dentistry, Prosthodontics, Periodontology, Preventive and Community, Paediatric dentistry, and Orthodontics.
House surgeon includes 8 departments: 1.

Oral and Maxillofacial Surgery, 2.
Oral Medicine, 3.
Conservative Dentistry, 4.
Prosthodontics, 5.
Periodontology, 6.
Preventive and Community Dentistry, 7.
Paediatric Dentistry, 8.
Orthodontics.
Maxillofacial surgery awaits those interested in surgery, treating jaw fractures and deformities.
Orthodontics is perfect for those who like aesthetics.
The Department of Prosthodontics covers Removable Prostheses, Maxillofacial Prostheses, and Dental Biomaterials in theory and practicals, and introduces Implant Dentistry.
Paediatric dentistry focuses on treating various child dental diseases and caring for children under 14.
Preventive and Community Dentistry is a fun subject for extroverts, involving short and long field trips starting from the fourth year to provide dental education.
In Oral Medicine, Oral Pathology and Radiology, you learn Dental X-rays and diagnose oral cancer versus regular tumors, essentially acting as a department that detects dental diseases.
Conservative Dentistry covers Fixed Prostheses, Tooth Preparation (JOC & SOC course), and Endodontics with modern clinical settings and simulators.

Periodontology teaches you about the health of gums and surrounding tissues and common diseases, providing both theory and clinical experience since you treat patients yourself. What activities are there at dental universities? The Htamane festival is the most fun, along with football, Fresher Welcome, Pathan chanting, university Thingyan, E-Sports Competition, Dental Observations, and TPPD Programs. How are exams conducted? There are 5 major exams: test 1, 2, 3, practical, and final test (theory + practical). Tests 1, 2, and 3 usually take 10%, practical 20%, and final 50%. This changes in senior years (e.g., in third year when handling patients, scores are combined, or some subjects without practicals take 70% for finals). Theory tests require memorizing a lot for 10-mark essay questions. Roll call warnings are strict; 7 students in our 2nd year batch were debarred. Although you can cheat roll calls in some subjects, most require you to raise your hand. For hostels, Yangon UDM does not provide a boys hostel, but there are many outside hostels. Mandalay UDM provides both boys and girls hostels. Priority is given to rural students, but Yangon students can also get hostel rooms. Honestly, I ended up in dental because I missed the medical cutoff by one mark. I wasn't interested at first, but handling patients and studying teeth made me interested. As a dentist, you have many opportunities if you work hard. Warmly welcome to all joining dental! Feel free to ask me anything if you need help or don't understand something.

Q: What was the total cutoff mark? Is it over 450, or do they only look at the combined 3 subjects? A: The total cutoff for Naypyitaw is 450, and then it's separated by ECB marks for medicine and dental.
Q: Do I apply for regular medicine first and then dental separately? A: You can rank up to 20 choices on the form. For example, first choice Medicine, second choice Dental.

Q: Do we have to sign a bond? A: Yes, you must sign a bond. After graduation, you must serve the government for 5 years and sign an agreement for it. If you don't work for the government and go abroad, you must pay 700 lakhs compensation. It depends on whether you attend through a private or government program. Private program students don't have to sign.
Q: Number of students accepted by the University of Dental Medicine in 2024? A: In 2024, UDM (Yangon) accepted 77 students and UDM (Mandalay) accepted 75 students.
Q: If I want to attend dental school, should I put medicine or dental first on the form? A: If medicine is your main priority, put it first. If dental is your main priority, put dental first.
Q: If I graduate from dental school and don't want to serve the 5-year government duty, paying the 700 lakhs means I no longer have to work for the government, right? A: Yes.
Q: When did the ECB system start being used? A: It started during the last 2 years of the old system.
Q: How much are the hostel and food costs for boys? A: It varies from hostel to hostel, but some are around 60,000, 80,000, or 100,000 kyats.
Q: Do you have to pay a fee for the girls' campus hostel? A: The girls' hostel is free, but you share meter and water pumping fees with your roommates.
Q: What are the class hours? A: For Year 1, it's from 9 AM to 4 PM (morning lectures, evening practicals). Year 2 has classes starting at 8 AM.
Q: Does the study load increase by the third year? A: Yes, second year is heavy too, but third year has a lot of studying and increased stress because you start handling patients.
Q: Are there outside tuitions available? How much do they cost? A: Yes, you can take tuition from house surgeons or OC. It costs around 40,000 to 50,000 kyats per subject.

Q: Do you handle patients starting from the third year? A: Yes, you handle patients in conservative dentistry in the third year. Before that, you must pass the JOC by practicing on phantom heads. You can only treat patients after passing JOC, which you must retake until you pass.
Q: Do you have to buy food outside yourself? A: You buy food from the school canteens.

Q: Will I be fine if I study day by day? I'm afraid of falling behind. A: There's a lot to study so you might fall behind. Don't worry too much, if you study well, you will pass. Many fail the tests, but if you study hard for the finals, you'll pass.
Q: How many times do they accept test failures before giving a warning? A: One bad thing is that if you fail exams, Student Affairs sends a letter to your home.
Q: Are tests once a month? A: Every two months, with tutorials and practical tests in between.

Q: Are the marks proportional to the effort required for tests? A: No, it's not proportional. Even if you struggle hard to get a passing mark of 50, they only take 10%, meaning you only get 5 marks out of the total 100.

Q: Do you become an intern right after uni for both medicine and dental? Do interns get paid immediately? A: Yes, after the final year, you become a House Surgeon and intern at hospitals and the university. Yes, a salary is paid.
Q: Is there a 50-bed dental hospital at the dental university? A: Yes, there is.
Q: Is getting treatment at dental school cheaper than outside? A: You only pay for the materials. Outside clinics charge doctor fees, but since they are dental students here, it is cheaper.
Q: Can you work at outside clinics to gain experience while studying (both Med and Dental)? A: Yes, for both. Next year, I'll work at an outside clinic as a trainee/assistant, not as a doctor.
Q: How do you manage that with class hours? A: You do it on holidays or after class hours to gain experience.
Q: Is a hostel available for Yangon UDM? A: Yes.
Q: How much are the living and food costs at Mandalay UDM? A: It depends on how much you spend. Campus hostels don't charge a room fee.
Q: Does the hostel provide food? What is the food cost? A: You eat at the canteen; cooking is not allowed. Costs depend on how much you eat, but eating frugally costs around 350,000 kyats.
Q: Sounds like food costs alone are quite high. A: Not necessarily, it depends on how you choose to eat.
Q: Is the hostel only for girls? Don't they provide one for boys? A: The hostel is only for girls; there is no boys' hostel. Edit: I'm referring to Yangon UDM.
Q: How many years is the house surgeon period for dental? A: House surgeon is one year. The required government duty after graduation is five years.
Q: How many years in total including school and house surgeon? A: School and house surgeon combined is 5 years under your system.
Q: What about a private clinic? A: You can open a private clinic after you graduate.
Q: Does the government job require full-time hours? A: You don't have to be there full-time. You just go during your required hours and can work at your private clinic during the rest of the time.
Also, if you know dentists outside, you can assist them while attending school.
Q: How much is the government salary? A: House surgeon gets 200,000 kyats. Graduates get over 300,000 kyats (maybe 310,000 + bonuses). It's not much different from regular medicine.
Major hospitals have a separate dedicated department for dental and oral medicine.

Q: Do you use a stethoscope in dental school? A: Yes, you use it in Year 1 for physiology practicals. Prices range from 10,000 to over 100,000 kyats. High-end brands are better for clearer sound if you plan to use it constantly.
Q: If we buy a stethoscope, is it only used for one year in First Year and rarely after? A: Yes, but in senior classes, the OS department uses it to measure blood pressure.
Q: Do you still have to serve the 5-year government duty after getting a postgraduate degree? A: Yes.
Q: So graduating requires 5 years, and taking a postgraduate degree also requires 5 years, it's the same? A: Yes.
Q: Are there tuitions? A: Yes, taught by school teachers or seniors. You can attend if you like, but self-study is fine too. Many seniors achieved distinctions without tuition.

Take your time to decide carefully on the university you want to attend for your future. Prioritize applying for what you are passionate about. Getting in or not is another matter, but also think of second plans just in case.
I heard you just apply to the 'University of Dental Medicine' and they assign you to a city later.
The ECB cutoff for boys is lower than for girls.
Boys have a higher chance in medical-related fields.
Medical applicants are first evaluated by their total marks, then the assessment committee ranks them by ECB from highest to lowest until the quota is reached.
Q: Are 'Dental Medicine' and 'University of Dental Medicine' the same? A: Yes.
Q: Is the cutoff mark the same nationwide? A: Yes, the cutoff is the same nationwide, and then they assign you to a city based on your exam center.
Q: How many copies of the mark transcript should I get? A: Get at least 5 copies.
Q: Why can't I apply to dental school with an NRC (Pru/Guest Citizen)? A: Authorities restricted it. Same for medicine; it used to be allowed but not anymore.

Q: If I submit the application to dental school and my total mark doesn't meet the requirement, will they turn me away? A: I heard Mandalay does. You are only allowed to apply if you have a full 450 marks.
Q: What is the photo size for the application form? A: Passport size.
Q: Are ward recommendation and health recommendation letters needed? A: No.
Q: Can the photo have glasses? A: Yes.
Q: Do I need to include NRC copies? A: Yes.
Q: I accidentally used a correction pen on the admission answer sheet, what should I do? A: It's fine to use it.
Q: For the form, should the passport photo be black & white or color? A: Color.
Q: How do I submit the form if I submit it after the deadline? A: You have to go to Yangon. If the date passes, send it to Thaton Road, Yangon.

Q: Do I have to pay compensation if I drop out of dental school due to various reasons like not being able to keep up? A: No need to pay if you haven't signed a bond. But rules change, so it's hard to be certain.
Q: dental 2024 cutoff score A: The Dental 2024 cutoff score shown is 388. {From MEDICINE 2024 (2).jpg}
Q: Which marks does Economics management look at? A: It looks at the Total marks.
Q: With a total of around 310, which economics-related university can I get into? A: You might get Statistics with 320-330. NOTE: This is an estimated answer and cutoffs can change yearly.
Q: With a total of 350, can I get into the Hlaing campus Economics University? A: No, you might not even make it into the Ywarthagyi campus. NOTE: Based on the previous year's situation.
Q: With a total of 451, is it enough for Hlaing Eco? A: Yes, it is enough.
Q: What major can I get at Ywarthagyi with around 350? A: You'll likely get the lowest major, which is BAS. NOTE: Based on last year's situation.

Q: What's the difference between the Business Management major at YUECO and other universities? A: YUECO is better. Dagon and others have economics, but YUECO degrees are more preferred. The NMDC BM is higher than YUECO and the curriculum is different.
Q: How many years does the Economics University take? A: 4 years.
Q: Does Economics Management have distance learning? A: Yes, distance learning for Economics Management is available at the Economics University.
INFO: Last year's cutoff was over 390 for Ywarthagyi and over 430 for Hlaing. NOTE: Hearsay/past year cutoff, not a current-year guarantee.

Q: Will it be okay to study Business Management if I graduated with a Biology combination? A: I graduated with Bio myself. It might be a bit difficult at first when encountering Eco theories, but it will be fine later on.
Q: Does Ywarthagyi have distance learning? A: Yes.
Q: Are the opportunities the same whether attending Hlaing or Ywarthagyi? A: Yes, they are the same, only the location differs. The degree awarded is identical.
Q: What clothes should be worn for the passport photo? A: Anything is fine, but a white shirt is preferred. The photo studio can edit it for you too.
INFO: Hlaing and Ywarthagyi cutoffs are not the same. Hlaing is higher. Past rumors noted Hlaing at 380/400+ and Ywarthagyi at 350/360. NOTE: Cutoffs vary by year.
Q: What kind of topics does BPA cover? A: Topics related to Public Administration, Trade, etc. It offers good job opportunities and high salaries in NGOs. Pursuing a Master's degree is recommended.
Q: What does BAS teach, and what are the job opportunities? A: It wasn't available during my year, so I don't know the details. It's a new major added later.

Q: What is the difference in degree/jobs between Economics distance learning and Day classes? A: Day classes are better. Employers prefer Day graduates, but distance learning is also good if you study well, and there are tuitions available.
Q: Can I get BBA in distance learning? A: Distance learning only has Economics and Business Management. BBA is only for Day classes. Business Management covers some BBA topics.
Q: Is 419 enough for BPA at Hlaing Economics? A: Yes, it is enough.
Q: What are the job opportunities with an Economics degree? A: You can get jobs in NGOs and government departments with good salaries. The salary varies based on your abilities.
Q: Which major is the most taken and most useful outside? A: The top majors are the most taken. I've heard that B.Com, B.Act, and BBA offer plenty of job opportunities.
Q: Does 440 qualify for all majors in Hlaing/Ywarthagyi? A: It qualifies for all Ywarthagyi majors. It might qualify for Hlaing, but Ywarthagyi is more certain.
Q: Is there a uniform for Economics? A: No. Girls can wear Myanmar traditional tops, and boys can wear longyis or long pants.
Q: Can I attend Economics at Hlaing with 382? A: No, you cannot.
Q: Do you get a job immediately after graduation? A: Yes, you get one depending on your effort.
Q: What was the cutoff for Bachelor of Commerce last year? A: Over 390. NOTE: Past cutoff, subject to change.
Q: Is Yangon Economics the highest? How is it different from Meiktila? A: Meiktila's cutoff is lower than Yangon's. Only the marks differ; the curriculum is exactly the same.
Q: Do you choose the major upon arrival, or put it on the application form? A: Put your preferred major on the form. The university assigns it based on your marks.
Q: Can biology combination students apply to the Economics University? A: Yes. I am a Bio student too.
Q: Is the Hlaing degree preferred over the Ywarthagyi degree? A: The degree received is the same, simply 'Yangon University of Economics'.
Q: Are there 6 majors in Economics? How do I choose? A: There are 9 majors, not 6.
Q: Does BBA include math? Are there majors without math? A: Maths is taught as a minor subject up to the 2nd year semester 1.
INFO: In previous years, cutoffs were 380 for Ywarthagyi and 420 for Hlaing, but they have decreased recently. NOTE: Historical reference.
Q: What is the difference between BBA and B.Com? A: B.Com covers both Accounting and HR, requires higher marks, and has more job opportunities.
Q: Can I take Economics via distance learning with Total 345 / Eco 81? A: Yes.
Q: Do I need to take LCCI, ACCA after a B.Com? A: Yes, it is necessary. I took LCCI and ACCA while at university and am now taking ACCA Part 2.
Q: What's the difference between entering work with just a training certificate vs a degree? A: There is a big difference. Companies prioritize degree holders.

Q: Do job opportunities differ between a YUECO degree and an NSA degree? A: YUECO is higher. NSA has lower cutoffs (accepts 300+), while Economics University requires at least 350. The curriculum might be similar.

Q: Do I need to include the major when applying? A: Write the campus name (e.g., Yangon University of Economics - Ywarthagyi). Prioritize the major you want regardless of whether your marks meet it. The assignment is based on your marks and your priority choice.
Q: Are local jobs rare for B.Com? A: Not very rare, but since there are many graduates, you must have strong skills. Taking a Master's is recommended.
Q: Can I transfer to Yangon after attending Meiktila Economics for one year? A: It's possible, but policies change yearly. NOTE: Verify with Student Affairs.
Q: Is there an Economics University in Mandalay? A: No, it must refer to Meiktila.
Q: What does BPA stand for? A: Bachelor of Public Administration.
Q: Can I submit the form via post? A: It's better to submit it in person. If it gets lost in the mail, you'll lose a year.

Q: Are Hlaing and Ywarthagyi listed separately? How should I write it? A: They are separate. Write 'Yangon University of Economics (Hlaing Campus)' or 'Yangon University of Economics (Ywarthagyi Campus)'.
Q: Are distance learning classes only on weekends? A: Yes.
Q: Where do I submit the university admission form? A: In Yangon, submit at Yangon University of Distance Education. In rural areas, submit at the local Arts/Science University.
Q: Must I submit the form for Economics in person? A: Yes, for Economics distance learning, you must submit it in person at our university, as it differs from Yangon Distance Uni.
Q: Do I need to include the passing certificate or a school recommendation? A: Not needed initially, provide them when the university requests them. NOTE: Check current year requirements.
Q: Is there a ferry from Hlaing to Ywarthagyi? A: Yes, or you can take 2 connecting buses.
Q: Can a Bio student catch up in Economics? A: Yes, you won't get lost, though it requires slightly more effort than Eco students. I am also a Bio student.
Q: Do I need to fill in the Day sections if applying for Distance? A: No.
Q: Does Yangon Economics only have Day classes, or is distance learning available? A: Distance learning is available (Economics and Business Management).
Q: Can I use a correction pen on the admission form? A: Yes, it is allowed.
INFO: Dagon University offers Arts/Science/Law degrees, while Economics universities offer Economics degrees. Distance Eco and BM are available at Yangon Eco (Ywarthagyi).
Q: What is the address to submit the form? A: Yangon University of Distance Education on Thaton Road, at the Agriculture bus stop.
Q: Does the university provide hostels and how much is it? A: Yes, it costs no more than 10,000 kyats per month.
Q: What is the average cost for Day classes? A: About 450,000 to 500,000 kyats a year for books, plus your personal expenses.
Q: YUECO vs NMDC, which is better? A: NMDC is slightly higher.
Q: Which campus is Economics distance learning located at? A: Ywarthagyi only. Hlaing is Day only.
Q: Do you receive a B.A. instead of a B.Econ for distance learning at Ywarthagyi? A: Yes.
Q: Do I have to memorize in English for Economics? A: Yes, university subjects are in English.
Q: Difference between Economics and Business Management? A: Business Management prioritizes management subjects.
Q: How do I fill in the township/region on the form? A: Write the township/region where you passed on one side, and your current residence on the other.
Q: Should I put only one priority university or multiple? A: Write at least 3 inside the papers and put your most desired one on the envelope.
Q: Is Economics only for Arts combinations or Science too? A: Yes, any combination can apply as per the guidelines.
Q: Can I attend distance learning at Hlaing or Kamayut? A: No, it's only available at Yangon University of Economics (Ywarthagyi Campus).
Q: What majors are available in distance learning? A: Only Economics and Business Management.
Q: How can I request another original marks transcript? A: You can print it online for a fee.
Q: Should DOB be written in numbers or words? A: Write it exactly as in your NRC.
Q: Should I include the major next to the priority university on the application? A: No need, just apply to the university.
INFO: Form documents require 6 envelope papers and 3 NRC copies (self/parents), totaling 9 papers. Passing certificate and transcript are separate and provided upon request.
Q: What can I apply for with under 300 marks? A: You can apply to the Co-operative University.
Q: Is 322 enough for Economics distance learning? A: Yes. Last year the cutoff was 303.
Q: Are NMDC BBM and Hlaing Economics degree the same? A: No, BBM is higher. Business Management focuses more on management.
Q: Is a total of 384 enough for Hlaing? A: No, but it is possible for Ywarthagyi.
Q: Can Biology combination apply for Economics? A: Yes, guidelines state any combination is eligible.
Q: What are the minimum marks to apply for Economics? A: Estimated 350 for Day (Ywarthagyi) and 330 for distance.
Q: Should I write the major when choosing the university? A: No, you apply for the major during enrollment.
Q: What is the most popular major with the highest cutoff at YUECO? A: B.Com is the most popular and usually has the highest cutoff, followed by BBA and B.Act.
Q: Can 409 make it to Hlaing, and what majors? A: Yes, last year the cutoff was 380+. The major depends on the applicants, but you might get lower majors. Top majors require around 450.
Q: Are majors assigned after joining? A: You don't write them initially; you re-apply for majors once admitted.
Q: Do I pick the major myself or does the university assign it by marks? A: You prioritize the major you want, and the university assigns it based on your marks. You cannot change majors later.
Q: Will 416 get into Hlaing campus? A: You might get into Hlaing for lower majors, but you could secure top majors at Ywarthagyi.
Q: Do they provide hostels for distance learning? A: Unheard of.
Q: Can I transfer to YUECO later if my marks meet the cutoff but I didn't include it in my form? A: No.
Q: Do all First Years get a hostel? A: Priority is given to rural students. Apply for the hostel as soon as enrollment opens.
Q: When are the classes and exams for distance Economics? A: Exams are estimated around October 13th.

Q: Universities of Economics available majors A: The listed economics-university majors are Bachelor of Commerce (B.Com), Bachelor of Accounting (B.Act), Bachelor of Business Administration (BBA), Bachelor of Public Administration (BPA), Bachelor of Development Studies (BDevs), Bachelor of Economics (Statistics) BEcon(Stats), Bachelor of Economics (Economics) BEcon(Eco), Bachelor of Population Studies (BPS), and BAS shown in the source photo as actual/actuarial science. {From YUECO (13).jpg}

Q: BCom major information and career paths A: B.Com is a commerce major and four-year degree. The source describes commerce together with accounting and management-related study, with possible continuation to M.Com and PhD.Com. Career paths mentioned include CFO, CPA, accounting, commerce, and finance-related work. {From YUECO (14).jpg}

Q: BAct major information and career paths A: B.Act is Bachelor of Accounting. The source says it is separate from Commerce and focuses on accounting, finance specialization, record/accounting work, and related business records. It is shown as a four-year degree with a master's path, and jobs are mentioned in banks and companies. {From YUECO (12).jpg}

Q: BBA major information and specializations A: BBA covers business organization, leadership, supply chain, decision science, and general management. Specialization areas shown include Financial Management, HR Management, Operations Management, and Marketing Management. Q: Where is UM2 located?

Q: How many years do you have to attend Medical University/UM2?
A: The school requires 6 years (Yr 1 to 5) + 1 year of House surgeon. Upon entry, you have to take a 1.5-month introductory course to get accustomed to medical terms and briefly study the subjects to be taught.

Q: What subjects are taught in the Introductory course?
A: In the Intro course, 6 subjects are taught: Anatomy, Physiology, Biochemistry, Pathology, Pharmacology, and Microbiology.

Q: What was the cutoff mark for UM2 admission last year?
A: Last year's cutoff mark was 231 for males and 243 for females. This refers to the combined score of the three subjects: English, Chemistry, and Biology.

Q: What do you learn in Year 1 and Year 2?
A: Yr 1 + 2 are pre-clinical classes. They are taught only at the school and are classes where you do not interact with patients. You learn 5 modules in each year. In Yr 1: Musculoskeletal and Skin, Genetic, Cardiovascular, Respiration, Gastrointestinal and Liver; in Yr 2: Haemato, Immunology, Endocrine, Renal and Repro, Neurology, etc.

Q: What is included in the medical school modules?
A: In each module, besides the 6 intro subjects, you also learn about a doctor's ethics such as CFH and Ethics, as well as current health activities being implemented in the Myanmar community.

Q: How are marks obtained and what is the exam format in Year 1/2?
A: In Yr 1/2, marks are obtained from Roll call, weekly assessments, presentations, and posters. There is 1 Test after completing each module, and all 5 modules must be taken again in the final exam.

Q: What is taught in Year 3?
A: Yr 3 is the year you really have to struggle. It includes 4 months of Medicine + 1 month of CFH + 1 month elective + 4 months of surgery. The 4 months of Medicine are further divided into 2 months of general and 2 months of specialty.

Q: What do you do in CFH?
A: During the 1 month of CFH, you have to draw lots and conduct a survey. You have to collect and study the family's lifestyle, vaccination, and diet patterns in the area you are assigned to.

Q: What is the 1-month elective?
A: The 1-month elective is free. You can apply back to the department you want to go to. Since clinical departments as well as school departments are available, it is explained that this month is easy going/chill.

Q: Should I apply to medical school if I get around 235 in CEB?
A: The answer given is "You can get in, try applying." An example is given that in years with difficult questions, the cutoff mark can drop, so there have been people who regretted not applying.

If you are going to attend at your own expense, you will be asked when you go take the entrance exam, and it is also included in the form.

Q: What do government expense, own expense, and bond mean in medical school?
A: A person attending at government expense signs a bond stating they will work as a government employee after graduation. If the bond is broken, it is answered as 600 lakhs. The government expense salary is just over 1000 per month, not 1000 lakhs. It is estimated that one's own expenses can reach into the hundreds.

Q: Do I have to include it right away in the application if I want to attend at my own expense?

1) Medical Laboratory Technology (MLT)
Medical Laboratory Technology

Simply put, it is a major where you research blood serum and other body fluids in a laboratory.
In this major, you will study blood testing and blood-related topics, methods of finding diseases from the body's flesh tissues, microorganisms beneficial to the body, and external microorganisms.

Good salary
Plentiful jobs
You can work in hospitals, clinics, medical companies, laboratories, and NGOs.
The downside is that you have to memorize a lot of texts😞

Q: What major is Prosthetic & Orthotics? A: Prosthetic & Orthotics is the artificial limbs supporting device major. It is a major that makes artificial limb control devices for people who have lost their limbs and those born with limb disabilities.

For the younger brothers and sisters who want to attend the Maritime University, I will share what I know about our university.
🤗 First of all, what kind of university is the Maritime University (MMU)?
What does it teach?
Most say that if you attend the Maritime University, you can board a ship as a graduate, become an officer, and get a good salary, merely for the sake of boarding a ship and getting money.
Actually, the Maritime University is much more than just boarding a ship.
**The Maritime University is the only university in the whole of Myanmar that teaches all advanced subjects related to maritime affairs.** ______________ ⚓ Majors within the university ⚓ All 7 majors, which act as the lifeblood of our great Maritime University, are crucial in their own right.
To trade and commute from one place to another across the sea, ships are needed for transportation.
The **Naval Architecture and Ocean Engineering (NA)** major, which teaches the advanced skills needed to build such ships, is indispensable.
A ship cannot simply exist as a mere hull; for a ship to operate, you must design and set up the essential engines and machinery.
Mechanical engineers who will build ships are required for this, and the **Marine Mechanical Major (MM)** is there for it.
Once a ship is built, people are needed to guide and steer it without tilting in the vast ocean so that it reaches its destination and keeps the transported goods safe.
Therefore, the **Nautical Science (NS)** major, which teaches the skills needed to steer and drive a ship, is also very important.
A ship is driven by machine power, and the machinery systems are incredibly complex.

To properly handle these important machines and engines, which serve as the ship's heart in the engine room, good Marine Engineers are needed.
Electrical and electronic engineers are absolutely necessary to expertly operate the electrical equipment, power distribution systems, automatic systems, and communication systems onboard.
The **Marine Engineering major (ME)** and **Marine Electrical System and Electronics Engineering major (MESE)**, which produce such good engineers, are also particularly important.
A ship cannot just keep sailing in the ocean forever.
They need a temporary rest at ports, which act as homes for the ships.
Therefore, ports serving as resting places are a necessity, and the **Port and Harbour Engineering (PH) major**, which teaches port maintenance and construction skills to systematically manage, maintain, modify, and build these ports, is extremely important.
When speaking of maritime, rivers, streams, and coastlines cannot be left out either.
The **River and Coastal Engineering (RC)** major, which teaches skills to protect the beauty and significance of rivers and coastlines from natural disasters and keep them sustainable, is also a very important major of our great university.
The great Maritime University was created so that you can study this entire maritime environment all in one place 🛳 _____________ 🎯 University Admission Guidelines 🎯 University admission marks vary from year to year depending on the number of applicants and the score levels.
Last year, it was restricted that you must have a minimum score of 420 and above to apply, and the actual final cutoff mark was 435.

If you want to be certain about your school admission, you should have around at least 450, and if you want to get your desired major, you need an average of around 470 or 480.
When applying to the school, besides meeting the specified marks, you also have to take entrance exams such as the Eye Test, IQ Test, and Psycho Test.
Medical checkups such as weighing, measuring height, testing for HIV, Hep B, Hep C, and taking an ECG are also required; even if your marks meet the standard, you cannot attend if you don't have the health conditions specified by the school.
(Colorblindness, HIV, Hep B, and Hep C cases are primarily checked) ______________ ⭐ Job Opportunities ⭐ Briefly describing the 7 majors, the NS major awards a B.Sc(Hons) degree while the other 6 majors award a B.E degree.
The main goal of the 3 majors NS, ME, and EE is to board a ship, and only boys are allowed to attend.
Those wearing glasses cannot attend the NS major.
As for job opportunities, you can board a ship as an officer, not as a bottom-tier staff; as a graduate, you'll have better chances when applying for jobs, you don't need to pay bribes, and if your performance is good, your promotion will be fast.
For NS, you can reach the highest rank of Captain; for ME, Chief Engineer (CE); and for EE, Electro Technical Officer (ETO) at the highest.
NS has better job opportunities onboard and you can become the Captain, the highest rank of the entire ship, but there are few jobs to do onshore.
(You can enter and work in office and teaching sectors).
ME and EE allow you to board ships and also transition back to working as Engineers onshore.

The 4 majors MM, PH, NA, and RC can be attended by both girls and boys, and job opportunities are plentiful in Asian countries like Japan and Singapore, as well as Western countries like Australia.
Most enter and work in port construction sectors, and domestically, they mostly work at MITT (Thilawa Port) and government offices.
If you want to pursue further study, you can attend school abroad and get a master's degree.
I also heard there are scholarship programs.
Top-ranking students are sometimes offered scholarships by major shipping companies.
If you try hard and are brilliant, you can also win scholarships and cash prizes ✨ Except for the NS major, the other majors award engineering degrees, so you can enter and work in many engineering-related jobs.
_____________ **In summary, MMU graduates have many job opportunities and many paths to earn foreign income ✨** Therefore, if you want to study advanced maritime knowledge, have an adventure in the vast ocean, work as a professional abroad, and become wealthy and a big shot at a young age without worrying about job opportunities, I invite you to come to our MMU.

How much will it cost to attend MMU???
This is the most frequently asked question right now: does it cost money to attend MMU?
Roughly how much does it cost per month???
How much does it cost before boarding a ship????
The first thing to clarify is that expenses vary from person to person; it depends on how much you spend, so I cannot give an exact amount.
Generally speaking, the mandatory initial costs include school enrollment fees, Uniform fees, and Shoe & Necktie fees.
The school enrollment fee is not that high, just around tens of thousands.
The exact amount will be posted by the Student Affairs during the enrollment season; it's an affordable amount for everyone, so it's not a problem.
The next thing is the Uniform issue, which is a problem.
A set of Ready-Made Uniform varies widely from around 50,000 to 100,000, and if you tailor it yourself, it can be around 40,000 to 80,000.
These are estimated prices; depending on the Quality you use, prices differ.
If you want it cheaper, there are white shirts sold for around 8,000 to 12,000 in Seconds or Export Outlets.
You can find good pants for around 10,000 to 18,000 if you know how to look; if you don't know where, just go to Kyimyindaing open-air market.
Shoes are quite expensive, starting from 60,000 and varying in price.
I've heard they will be mandatory to wear in the coming years, so you will definitely have to buy a pair.
There are cheaper ones, but they are very bad; your feet will hurt and the Quality is quite poor.
Next will be the Necktie fee and Badge logo fee, which are not very expensive, just around 10,000.
After these, for students coming from outside the city, there are hostel fees.
The inner hostel is cheaper; hostel fee is 10,000 + 88,000 for dinner.

For outside hostels, it varies; the main one where students stay is the Kant Kaw hostel, which is 50,000 per month without food provided.
If you buy food, it's 4,500 per meal.
Food costs are not included in the cheaper school canteens.
Whatever you eat, rice and curry cost 4,000.
If you take an extra portion, it's 5,000.
If you know how to drink cold drinks and smoke a cigarette, it will go up to around 7,000 a meal.
So, food expenses depend on your own appetite.
In Kant Kaw, you can manage by secretly cooking rice with a small red pan or rice cooker and frying eggs.
Another mandatory expense is buying Drawing materials.
Since it's Basic Essential, every engineer must buy it.
The school SC usually collects everything and orders them at a cheaper price than the outside market rate, so it is cheaper than outside.
You don't have to go buy them, which saves you trouble.
But there are some issues like getting odd lengths like Wukong's staff.
The cost depends on the major and the Quality you use, ranging up to around 30,000 to 70,000.
Now you can calculate the rough expenses.
I didn't include Text Book costs in this because you don't have to buy them.
Since there are pdf files, if you don't want to spend extra, you can just skip buying them.
Even if you want to buy them, the prices vary, so it's not convenient to mention each one.
So, combining living costs, hostel fees + food + Data fees, etc., you need at least a mandatory minimum of around 300,000 a month just to get by.
If you cook for yourself and are frugal, it can be cheaper, and if you spend more, it will cost more, so I advise you to judge for yourself.
If you have further questions, you can ask.
I will talk separately about what courses you need to attend and how much it costs before boarding a ship.

**University Of Pharmacy **
Hello younger brothers/sisters, I am Ma Mi Syke Pakao, a Mon ethnic girl, currently in my Final Year (1/2025) from the 28th batch of the University of Pharmacy, Yangon.
First of all, Congratulations to the little Jujus who passed G12!
Since the score sheets are out now, you must all be excited about the university you want to attend.
That's why I'm going to share some information about what you should know regarding the University of Pharmacy.
1.
Firstly, what is the University of Pharmacy?
The University of Pharmacy is not very popular among people in Myanmar yet; some still confuse it with pharmacy technicians (pharmacist aids) even now.
Actually, the University of Pharmacy is a great university that produces very talented and brilliant Pharmacists.
People in this country might not know Pharmacists well, but internationally, everyone respects them as soon as they hear 'Pharmacist'.
Little Jujus will understand why after arriving at this school.
If I have to tell you the minimum cutoff marks by year:
• 2018/2019 - Male/Female - 482
• 2019/2020 - Male/Female - 421
• 2021/2022 - Male/Female - 452
• 2022/2023 - Male/Female - 427
• 2023/2024 - Male/Female - 472
• 2024/2025 - Male/Female - 388
• 2025/2026 - Male/Female - Not out yet.
2.
Where is the school located?
There are two universities of pharmacy in Myanmar: Yangon and Mandalay.
The Yangon Campus is located in Upper Okkalapa Township, Dhamma Thukha Street, right next to the Waibargi Infectious Disease Hospital.
The Mandalay Campus is located in Amarapura Township, near Taw Twin Village, near the Htone Bo-Myitnge bypass, next to the Mental Health Hospital.
3.
How many years do we have to attend the school, and what subjects do we have to study?
You have to attend the school for four years.

Unfortunately, only students who passed with the Biology combination are allowed to join.
There are no further major divisions in the school; everyone studies the same subjects.
After graduating, you will receive a Bachelor of Pharmacy (B.Pharm) degree.
I will explain in detail below what career opportunities will come after graduation.
For now, I will first tell you the subjects to be studied year by year.
In the First year, as Major subjects:
1.
Pharmaceutics (Pharmacology formulation science)
2.
Pharmaceutical Chemistry (Pharmaceutical chemistry)
3.
Pharmacognosy (Traditional medicine science)
And as Minor subjects:
4.
Anatomy
5.
Physiology
6.
Biochemistry
7.
Myanmar Language
8.
English Language
9.
Behavioral Science
So there are 9 subjects.
In the Second year:
As Major subjects:
1.
Pharmaceutics
2.
Pharmaceutical Chemistry
3.
Pharmacognosy
4.
Pharmacology
As Minor subjects:
5.
Physiology
6.
Biochemistry
So there are 6 subjects to study.
In the Third year:
As Major subjects:
1.
Pharmaceutics
2.
Pharmaceutical Chemistry
3.
Pharmacognosy
4.
Pharmacology
As Minor subjects:
5.
Pathology
6.
Medical Microbiology
7.
Pharmaceutical Microbiology
So there are 7 subjects.
And in the final year, Final year:
1.
Pharmaceutics Paper 1
2.
Pharmaceutics Paper 2
3.
Pharmaceutical Chemistry
4.
Pharmacognosy
5.
Pharmacology
So you will study the major subjects in detail.
(If you want to know about each subject, you can ask further.
In the current new system, the subjects taught by year vary slightly, but they are the same subjects.)
4.
What kind of people should join?

In particular, it is best for people who are genuinely interested in pharmaceutical science and want to become a Pharmacist to join.
This is because the texts are difficult and very advanced, and furthermore, in an undeveloped country like Myanmar, full facilities and support for Pharmacy like in other countries cannot be provided, so students have to learn mostly theory, which presents some difficulties for students (a gap between Theory and Clinical).
But there's no need to worry, you can get through it smoothly.
The main thing is that it's better if you can maintain the study flow from Grade 12.
Does that mean if you lack passion, it becomes a wrong choice?
No, it's not.
Those who missed the medical school cutoff score and ended up at this school are now happy in this career.
This is because almost all subjects taught in medical school are taught as minor subjects at this school.
Another thing is that there are Labs & Practicals, so you will proudly wear a Duty Coat.
Also, although there are many exams, you will get used to it later.
If you want a B.Pharm, you have to memorize enough text worthy of a B.Pharm.
5.
Are there campus activities?
Although there are many exams, there are also festive events.
There is the Fresher Welcome event to welcome you younger brothers/sisters (Warmly Welcome, everyone!), the Fund Fair which is an on-campus sports event, the on-campus Water Festival event in March, the Teacher Homage ceremony, and Farewell, which is an event to say goodbye to the Final Year seniors.
You can participate in these events (singing, dancing, playing instruments).

In recent years, as an additional event, there is a medical and allied health boys' football match involving all schools under the Ministry of Health (5 Medical Schools, Pharmacy, Nursing, Medical Technology, Basic Health).
It's a huge event that you can watch thoroughly and also participate in.
6.
Is hostel accommodation provided for regional students?
On-campus hostels are available for both boys and girls.
However, because there are many females at UOPY, in our time, we had to stay in an off-campus hostel for the first month and got the campus hostel only after that.
But now that new hostels have been constructed, I don't know if you will get it right away.
For boys, they get the hostel right away.
If you ask how many people have to live in a room, currently there are three female hostels, which are three-story buildings.
One hostel has just finished construction and hasn't opened yet.
We lived in Padonmar Hostel (old hostel).
There are 56 rooms in total, with 2 people per room.
Another old hostel is shared with teachers, so 5 people are placed in one room (apartment style).
Boys are also placed 2 people per room.
The hostel fee is 10,000 Kyats per month, which totals 120,000 Kyats for a year.
* 24-hour electricity, 24-hour Wi-Fi are available.
7.
Job Opportunities
No need to worry about employment.
You can start working the day after graduation.
The demand for Pharmacists in Myanmar is very high.
Although it is very high, because the government cannot support Pharmacists as much as doctors, the Pharmacist role has not flourished much in Myanmar yet.
After graduation, you can enter four fields: 1.
Academic, 2.
Clinical, 3.
Industrial, 4.
Community.
1.
If you have a passion for teaching, you can work as a teacher at the university across various departments.
2.

For Clinical, you will have to work in a hospital as a Clinical Pharmacist.
You will have to counter-check the medications prescribed by doctors to patients, and you also give patient counseling.
However, Clinical Pharmacy does not exist in government hospitals yet; you only work as a Hospital Pharmacist.
This involves managing and purchasing medicines at the Dispensary, and dispensing medicines prescribed by doctors.
3.
Industrial Pharmacist - This involves manufacturing medicines at pharmaceutical factories.
Pharmaceutical factories highly value Pharmacists because manufacturing medicines cannot happen without Pharmacists.
The rank is also high; you can even be called the King & Queen of the entire factory.
It is really interesting.
4.

Community Pharmacist - This involves working at the Food and Drug Administration, known as the FDA. Inside it, it is further divided into various departments. For example, Cosmetics, Food, Regulatory, etc. You have to work on registration in these departments. The public is only allowed to use a medicine brand or a food type, etc., after we Pharmacists approve it.

If there is anything else you want to know, you can ask me back. Good luck, kids!

Q: Total 410 / ECB 278 / Can I get Pharmacy (YGN), brother?
A: If your score is over 400, you can apply. You might get in if few people apply, younger brother.

Q: Can I apply if my Total is 414 / EBC is 209? / After studying for 4 years, do I have to serve the state? / Can I work in foreign hospitals after getting this degree? / How is the university admission form filled? Do I have to list down all the universities I want to attend?
A: You have to write 10 / After writing your preferences, you must fill 10 / If your score is over 400, you can apply, younger sister. After graduating, you have to serve the government for 5 years. While working for the government, you can work part-time at private hospitals and clinics. You cannot practice as a professional Pharmacist abroad with just a B.Pharm from this country; additional requirements specified by each country are needed, such as taking a Pharmacist license exam, FPGEE (Foreign Pharmacy Graduate Equivalency Exam), English Fluency Test, etc. As far as I know, for example in Australia, you have to attend OSPAP (Overseas Pharmacist Assessment Program) for one year and complete another 52 weeks of internship. Only after that can you officially work as a Pharmacist in their country. This is because developed countries no longer have a four-year Pharmacy course; it is a 6-year course called PharmD (Doctor of Pharmacy). Here, let alone the PharmD program, we haven't even got a Clinical Pharmacy department in hospitals yet.

INFO: That's why I want you to think carefully before applying for Pharmacy. The Pharmacy role in this country, especially the Clinical part, is not yet developed. You get a B.Pharm, but the tasks a B.Pharm holder has to perform in a hospital are slightly incompatible. Also, there is a lot of text to memorize. If you can memorize well, it's for the best.

Q: Since I'm only interested in medical-related fields, what kind of school should I choose? My score isn't very satisfying either.
A: If you are interested in medicine, you will study Pharmacology in depth in Pharmacy. I also like Pharmaco; it covers classes of drugs, mechanism of action of drugs, how they are used for which diseases, drug interactions, benefits and side effects of drugs, etc. / Pharmacology is the life of Pharmacy; you can say it's a Main subject. It is also taught in other medical schools and allied health fields, but people say we go into more detail.

Q: If it's about my passion, I want to choose UOPY, sister. But regarding the career, I'm afraid my family might not agree. Even though I want to follow the plan laid out by my family, I want to continue my schooling in Myanmar. If only the salary is good in Myanmar, it would be quite fine. I'm just praying that it goes well.
A: The Pharmacist job has a good salary in the private sector, younger sister. Rather than what your family tells you to attend, I want you to apply for what you are passionate about, younger sister. If your passion and career don't match, I'm afraid you will be exhausted, so please think carefully.

Q: Sister, if I got 392, would it be okay?
A: I can't say for sure, younger sister. Last year, they went down to 388 to call students. Good luck!
NOTE: Previous-year cutoff reference only; not a current-year guarantee.

INFO: In the government sector, they only pay the current government salary. Job opportunities in the private sector include working as a Pharmacist at private hospitals, clinics, Medicare, lifeplus, shwe ohh pharmacy, etc. The salary is 7 digits and is only bound to increase. Of course, you need to build up a Pharmacist's ability.

INFO: Plus, you can work as medical representatives in pharmaceutical companies, and the salary for this is even better. As industrial Pharmacists, you can work at private pharmaceutical factories like YSI and Fame.
INFO: Hospitals hire Pharmacists more often than private clinics do.

INFO: Just attend the school happily, kids. If you have the skills, money will automatically follow you. With Pharmacist ability, you deserve an even higher salary than that. To be worthy of the texts you memorized and the degree you earned, we must raise the role of Pharmacy in this country. That's why when it reaches your generation, I would like to ask for help to strive to establish a clinical pharmacy department in hospitals, even if a PharmD program isn't available yet. A lot of your strength is needed.

Q: What is clinical posting like?
A: Clinical posting means going to the hospital and interacting with patients. You ask about the patients' medical history, record what medications are used for which diseases in notes, and present them back to the doctors. They will teach you in return.

Q: Can you tell me what the basic salary status is like for most industrial pharmacists and community pharmacists, sister?
A: The salary of Industrial and Community Pharmacists in the government sector is the same as a Hospital Pharmacist. The salaries of all government employees are over 200,000 Kyats, younger brother. If it's at pharmaceutical factories like Fame and YSI, I think it starts from 1,000,000 Kyats and above, and then the salaries increase gradually based on experience.

Q: How much does it cost per month to attend school?
A: Regarding how much it costs per month, first, when you initially enroll, there will be book fees. You have to buy books once per semester, and it can cost around 150,000 to 200,000 Kyats (this is my estimate, it might cost more than that). Also, enrollment fees, lab fees, and everything combined totals 12,000 Kyats. The rest depends on how much you spend; including tuition fees and food expenses, it comes to about 400,000 or 500,000 Kyats a month.

INFO: When I say you can, it means you can work part-time in the private sector while doing government work. The sisters who have graduated now are doing exactly that, child, and they say it's fine. Because some hospitals adjust working hours, they receive the full government salary in addition to a private full-time salary.

Q: Is there an opportunity in private hospitals for boys as well? I only see girls in the Pharmacy department.
A: Of course there is, everyone is a B.Pharm holder. The reason you don't see many boys might be because they don't work in hospitals. Since 80% of the students at school are girls, you mostly see female Pharmacists.

Q: Sister, when taking exams at Uni, is it like what we currently do in High School where, except for main points and spelling contained in the texts, we have to memorize and answer exactly paragraph by paragraph as written in the given book? Community.

I am a 4th-year senior sister from UIT, Kyi Sin Latt. Currently, I am majoring in Software Engineering at UIT. UIT is a university that mainly specializes in and teaches IT fields. Therefore, who should attend? It's a school that kids interested in IT programming and computers should attend. The location of the school is on Parami Road, diagonally opposite Hlaing ECO.

For UIT, under the old system, we had to attend for 5 years (a total of 10 semesters), but for your new system, you only need to attend for 4 years (8 semesters). You can start applying for UIT if your total score is 450. The cutoff mark for the previous year was a minimum of 459. For our 2019-2020 batch, the cutoff was 485, and only a total of 200 people are accepted per year. UIT does not look at the combined scores of the four specific subjects. As long as the total is 450, you are allowed to start applying.

Courseworks might vary from year to year, especially between the old system and the new system. However, regarding the major list to choose from, in our 3rd Year, we split into Bachelor of Computer Science and Bachelor of Computer Technology, and in the 4th Year:

Under Bachelor of Computer Science, there are:
- Software Engineering
- Knowledge Engineering (AI)
- Business Information Systems
- High Performance Computing

Under Bachelor of Computer Technology, there are:
- Communications and Networking
- Embedded Systems
- Cyber Security

So you have to choose one major out of these 7 majors. Since you are under the new system, you might have to choose a year earlier. For your year, they said the major you want to take will be decided by GPA, so when you get the university exam score sheet, you will need to calculate your own GPA based on the grades.

The lessons at school do not require memorizing and calculating to the point of mental and physical exhaustion. However, because UIT places great emphasis on projects (mainly because we have to participate in competitions frequently), I advise you to work on projects thoroughly and with focus. Many internship companies won't look at how much you know from books, but rather at which project and which part you took responsibility for and how you did it. Experiences from joining competitions will carry over to your resume in the future. As for exams, mid-terms, tutorials, final exams, and Moodle quizzes are usually conducted at UIT. Since marks are taken from these as well as from assignments, roll call, and projects, your GPA can be good if you study regularly at UIT.

From my personal experience, the school has a lot of exchange programs, project shows, and competitions. Since invitations come from both overseas and locally, I want you to enter and compete to gain experience. As for me, I have participated and contributed in activities such as:
- 2022 AYLP Cambodia
- 2023 AYLP Indonesia
- 2024 ACCF as a Liaison
- AAET Engineering Science Fair 2024
These are called for every year via the student mailbox. Although I didn't participate, Capture-The-Flag (CTF) events and Hackathons are called for every year. Regarding events and activities, while the above-mentioned events are frequently called by UIT, the rest are just the Monsoon Sports Festival and the Fresher Welcome event.

For regional people, female hostels and male hostels are provided. For boys, the hostel fee is 20,000 Kyats per semester; for girls, it is 60,000 Kyats per semester. The food fee is 150,000 Kyats per month. For university enrollment, both hostel students and outside day-students have to pay 65,000 Kyats per year.

Regarding opportunities after graduation, UIT provides plenty of opportunities. Among my seniors, there are those who did internships in Japan and got jobs, as well as those who interned at Myanmar-based Japanese companies and went back to work in Japan. One thing to mention is that to get such overseas internships, the school requires taking the ITPEC IP/FE exams, or N5/N4 Japanese exams. Classes for these are opened every year, and UIT students can attend them as extracurriculars at school. Currently, UIT also teaches Korean Language as an extracurricular at school. Since Master's degrees are taught at UIT and there are also recruitment offers from other countries, any opportunity is abundant at UIT. So, if you are passionate about programming and interested in IT, I recommend attending UIT. May the future journey of all younger brothers and sisters be smooth!

Seeing this message, let me explain it back. It's not that you will get a job only if you qualify. All of you must do an internship mandatory. This is already in the school curriculum, stating that you must intern in this semester and pass only when the company gives a certificate. Most people who do internships get jobs back, especially kids who intern in Japan. It's not just for the 'Qualified' ones. This is because for you to come to Japan, the company has to cover your expenses. To be worth that cost, you must work for them. Not getting a job only happens if you have trouble with superiors, have poor relationships at work, or things like that. One thing is, for interning in Japan, you can go if you take and pass N5/N4 and FE. Local internships do not require those.
Q: Will it be split based on High School matriculation scores, sister? A: No, it will be split based on the GPA scores obtained from taking exams at UIT, sister.
Q: What needs to be done for the UIT entrance? A: UIT does not have an entrance exam, sister. It will only look at the total Grade 12 score.
Q: What is the admission score, brother? A: You can apply starting from 450. As for the admission, the cutoff mark varies from year to year, so last year it closed at 459.

A: It's not just for first years, sister; it is given to second years and fourth years. It doesn't look at G12 scores, but only at the GPA from university exams, sister. You can start applying if your GPA is 3.5, sister.

Q: Is there a major with good job opportunities and income, brother? A: In Computer Science, although SE is general, most majors have good income both locally and abroad, sister. In Computer Technology, networking professionals have more abundant opportunities, sister. For ES and Cybersecurity professionals, both income and jobs will be more abundant abroad than locally, sister.

Q: I applied for UIT as my second preference. If I don't get my first preference, will I be sent to UIT, sister? If you don't meet the first, then the second; if not the second, then the third; if not the third, then the fourth. It goes just like that.

Q: Does UIT close for only two months a year? A: It closes for only 2 months, sister. You attend a semester for 4/5 months and it closes for 2 months. Since you have to attend 2 semesters a year, it closes for 4 months in a year.

Q: If I attend UIT and buy a personal laptop, what kind of model would be good? I can only afford a moderate one, or will it cause difficulties without a laptop, sister? A: A laptop is not an absolute must-buy. You can also go use the computer lab at school. You can buy it later when you are in senior years and want to do projects at home. Q: Do we have to wear a Uniform at UIT, sister? A: Yes, it's white and blue, brother. Except for sleeveless and low-cut necks, you can tailor it any style you like, sister. Kingfisher green is acceptable, as well as greenish-blue or blue, sister. Deep blue wasn't allowed before. Yes, that's right, sister.
Q: What are the school hours, sister? A: It is from 8:30 to 4, sister. As for whether class hours are full-time, we have to check after getting the timetable, sister.

Q: In an IT career, what is the maximum salary one can get locally? I'm really interested in that.
A: IT is broad. Even for a Software Engineer, the salary varies depending on whether it's frontend, backend, or fullstack. In addition to that, there are technical-focused roles like system engineer, cloud eng, devops eng., as well as roles mixed with business domains like business analyst. Fresh graduate with no skill (those who only do schoolwork) - 3.5 to 5
Fresh graduate with skills - 6 to 10
Exp around 1 to 2 years - 10 to 15
Exp above 3 years mid level - ~20
Senior - 20+
Team lead - around 30

Intended for younger brothers and sisters who are going to apply for university admission, I want to tell you about the University of Veterinary Science.
I am Ma Myo Thuzar Kyaw, a third-year DVM student studying veterinary medicine at the University of Veterinary Science 🍀.
The University of Veterinary Science is located in Yezin, Nay Pyi Taw, and it is the only school of its kind in the entire country of Myanmar.
Colloquially known as 'Ti-Mway-Ku' (Vet/Animal Husbandry), its official name is the University of Veterinary Science, and it is abbreviated as 'Mway/Say', UVS, or Vet.
The degrees offered are:
=======
Bachelor of Veterinary Science - DVM (5-year course) and Bachelor of Animal Science - BASc (4-year course).
Admissions will be called through newspapers, and if you plan to apply, you should submit your form as early as possible.
The deadline, set score, form format, and required documents will be announced in newspapers.
As soon as it is announced, it will be posted on this Union Page.
You can come to submit the form at the school yourself, or you can apply by sending it through the post office.
Applicants wishing to apply to the university must have passed the matriculation exam with Biology.
For the Bachelor of Veterinary Science - DVM, a total score of 410 and above must be obtained, and for the Bachelor of Animal Science - BASc, a total score of 380 and above must be obtained.
The scores change from year to year.
The location of the university is near Yezin Village, Zayyar Thiri Township, Nay Pyi Taw Federal Territory.
It is situated beside the old Yangon-Mandalay Road.

The western side of the school faces the Bago Yoma mountain range, and the eastern side faces the Shan Yoma mountain range, so the school's location can be affectionately called 'Alel Yoma' (the Middle Range).
Furthermore, the University of Veterinary Science is not the only institution on the campus; two other universities, Yezin Agricultural University and University of Forestry and Environmental Science, are also co-located there.
All three universities provide government hostels to every attending student.
You have to pay 25,000 Kyats per semester block for hostel fees, library fees, and exam fees combined.
The food fee is 10,500 Kyats per month, which covers two meals: lunch and dinner.
There are 6 hostels at Mway/Say: 2 male hostels (Ziwaka, Hanlin) and 2 female hostels (Manzoo, Gandamar).
In every hostel, 2 people share a room.
In the older hostels like Manzoo and Ayeyarwady, each student is provided with a bed, a writing desk, and a chair.
In the new hostels like Ziwaka, Hanlin, and Gandamar, each student is provided with a bed, a writing desk, a chair, a three-tier wardrobe, a fan, and a clothes drying rack.
Since last year, the G12 curriculum is no longer like what we studied.
In the first year, first semester, you have to study Myan, Eng, Maths, Physics, Chemistry, Botany, and Zoology, and take the final exam right away.
Practical exams and viva exams must also be taken.
In the second semester, you start learning medical-related subjects: Anatomy, Histology, Biochemistry, Physiology, Ethology, and English, making a total of 6 subjects.
In an academic year, you attend for (8) months (4 months for the first half + 4 months for the second half), and exams are held after each half.

The first assessment is called the First Semester/Mid-term Exam, and you answer for 25 marks.
The second assessment is called the Second Semester/Final Exam, and you have to take a written exam (for 40 marks), an oral exam (for 10 marks), and a practical exam (for 10 marks) over an exam period of nearly 1.5 months.
5 marks are given for attending Practicals and submitting Records, and the Tutorial test for 10 marks will be taken based on the average calculated for the whole academic year.
For practical learning, the university has its own Research Center, and there are school Lab rooms, a Veterinary Teaching Hospital (University Hospital), a demonstration poultry farm (broiler + layer), a demonstration beef cattle farm, a demonstration dairy cattle farm, a demonstration pig farm, and a demonstration pasture plantation.
Depending on the year, field study trips are organized, and study and treatment-related field trips are conducted by the Clinical Studies Association (CSA) and Livestock Studies Association (LSA) for field learning.

With the aim of gaining work experience before graduating from the university, final-year DVM students and final-year BASc students are required to do internships at the Livestock Breeding and Veterinary Department, veterinary clinics, livestock farms, and other related businesses to improve the students' quality.
Among campus activities, in addition to sports competitions, night markets, and festivals, because 3 universities are meeting together as the only school of its kind nationwide, various ethnic groups are gathered.
Thus, National Day commemorative ethnic traditional variety dance performances are held every year.
For the Fresher Welcome, a grand main welcome is organized, and state/regional welcomes are also held for the respective states and regions from which students came to attend.
Now that we've talked about what happens while attending school, I'd like to continue talking about the job opportunities available after graduation.
Even in the final year before graduation, 1) Livestock companies and 2) Pharmaceutical companies come to introduce themselves and recruit.
You can also work at 3) Agricultural, livestock, public health, and food-related companies, 4) NGO organizations, and 5) Government departments.
6) You can also work at Nay Pyi Taw, Yangon, and Mandalay Zoos.
7) If you enter a ministry, they directly grant the position of Government Deputy Officer.
8) You can enter the Veterinary and Slaughterhouse Departments of Nay Pyi Taw, Yangon, and Mandalay Municipalities as a Deputy Veterinary Officer, or 9) enter the Forest Department as an Elephant Doctor.
10) If you enter to serve in the military, you will start from the rank of a two-star lieutenant.
11) Based on your skills, you can also open your own private clinic to treat animals.

12) The Environmental Conservation Department directly recruits as an executive officer for wildlife protection-related work.
13) There are many other jobs related to the degree, so it can be said that there is no need to worry about being unemployed or searching for a job after graduation, since it can provide jobs for up to 100% of graduates.
This is a brief summary of what you should know about the University of Veterinary Science.
When choosing the university where you will spend your teenage years and which will shape your career, I urge you to choose only what you are passionate about, and choose the subject you are interested in, not because of your parents' pressure.

Hello everyone,

🏫Location
-WYTU university is located in Hlaing Tharyar Township.
🎓Majors
-There are a total of 11 majors to study at WYTU.
Those majors are:
1.Architecture
2.Electrical Power Engineering
3.Mechatronic Engineering
4.Information Technology
5.Mechanical Engineering
6.Electronic Engineering
7.Chemical Engineering
8.Textile Engineering
9.Civil Engineering
10.Metallurgy Engineering
11.Agricultural Engineering.

🎉Clubs and Activities
At West Yangon Technological University, known as WYTU, there are clubs such as:
-Buddhist Family
-Rakhine Family
-Literature Club
-English Club
-Sports Club
-Chess Club
-Cheerleader Club
-Arts Association
-Literature Association, making a total of (9) clubs.
Anyone interested from various majors can participate in all 9 of these clubs.🥳🥳

led by the Literature Club, book sales festivals, literature talks are held, and weekly knowledge sharing is provided.

Hello everyone 😄😄 What I am introducing now is Mechanical engineering.
Mechanical engineering in Burmese means engineering related to machinery.🔩🛠🔧 So, designing, producing, driving, and maintaining machines is what a Mechanical Engineer does.

In Solid, you will have to learn basic subjects like 👉 Mechanics of materials, 👉 Strength of materials, and 👉 Theory of machines....
👉 Also, when creating and producing a product, you will learn approaches and methodologies through practical projects in subjects like mechanical engineering design.

Mechanical subjects are practical.

INFO: Electrical Power Engineering (EP)
YTU 2024-2025 Admission
Male - E,M,C,P 334 Total 481
Female - E,M,C,P 322 Total 481
⚡Electrical Power is an engineering subject that studies electricity, which everyone must use nowadays.
Imagine a world without electricity.
How difficult would it be to survive.
Without electricity, there is no reason for humanity to have progressed this far.
That is why Electrical Engineering is such an important engineering subject.
⚡Mainly, EP has three fields: Power system part, Renewable part, and Machine control part.
When you enter the workplace, you can choose to work in the field you are skilled at.
⚡Do you know how the electricity we use at home gets there?
It comes through 3 main processes.
Those processes are Generation, Transmission, and Distribution.
In the EP major, you will learn in detail how those 3 work.
That part is called the Power system part.
⚡Currently, there is something becoming popular on a nationwide scale.
That is installing solar panels at homes.
Installing solar panels here doesn't mean you can just buy and install it however you want.
It must be installed systematically by calculating the load to be used at home and the amount of watts you will get from the solar panel.
In the EP major, you will also learn how to generate electricity from Solar energy, as well as Hydro and Wind.
That part is called the Renewable energy part.
⚡Everyone knows what a Transformer is.
The massive voltage sent from power stations has to be reduced by transformers to reach the single phase 230V we use at home.
In the EP major, you will learn how transformers work, about electrical machines, and how to control them.
That part is called the machine control part.

⚡In addition to the 3 main parts, EP students also have to study lighting systems of buildings, High voltage, and electrical safety.
According to Harvard University ranking, Electrical engineering is among the most difficult Engineering majors.
However, there is no need to be scared; since you have to apply a lot of your own reasoning and theories, having a good quick-wittedness will be beneficial for you.
If you are someone who has been interested in electricity since young, and loves tinkering with devices, then the EP major is highly suitable.
⚡In terms of Job opportunities, EP is a major with abundant opportunities.
Since electricity is a major required factor in Myanmar, you can work in projects run by the government or external private businesses.
As an advantage of EP, those who graduate with EP can work as seafarers with a good salary position by just attending a few months of courses.
You can also work as M&E Engineers in Companies.
The main thing is that you need to be proficient in the academic subjects from your end, concluding with this.
“We will make electricity so cheap that only the rich will burn candles.”
--Thomas Edison--
