INFO: Metallurgy Engineering
In the Metallurgy part, there are mainly two parts: extractive and adaptive.
In the Extractive part, using pyro (fire), hydro (liquid), and electrometallurgy methods, it involves purifying ores and metal mixtures to get exactly what is desired.
Usually, jobs in these parts are mostly located near mining areas.
It's like purifying what is extracted from the mine.
For example, processing rocks containing gold in various ways to extract the desired gold.
Adaptive Metallurgy is the science of creating and shaping the obtained types of metals into desired objects.
Car parts, household spoons, forks, steel used to build spacecraft, and parts making up metals used in phones are included.
Besides Metallurgy, within Materials science, you also have to study other carbon materials like graphene, and parts making semiconductor materials.
It also includes Nanotechnology and the characterization part which studies Materials.
The Majors are taught starting in 2nd yr.
In 2nd yr, introduction to metallurgy and introduction to materials science are taught generally.
As a Minor, you have to take inorganic chemistry.
In 3rd yr, you learn mineral processing which falls under extractive metallurgy, and the adaptive parts like foundry and solidification.
The Mineral processing subject roughly covers the detailed processes of separating and purifying valuable minerals and gangue minerals from ores obtained from mines, and various crushing processes to achieve the desired state.
Simply put, the foundry subject is about casting (melting metals and pouring them into a desired mold).
In this subject, you learn in detail about mold making and pattern making to pour molten iron.
You also learn how to pour the molten metal and what defects can occur.

Solidification is the subject that studies cooling melted liquid metals into solids.
It involves studying in-depth how to prevent defects during the cooling of the metal liquid poured in the earlier casting.
Then, as other majors, physical chemistry, heat transfer, fluid flow, and physical metallurgy (the subject studying the microstructures of metals) are taught.
As Minors, modern physics, strength of materials, mining minerals and environmental safety, mineralogy and petrology must be taken.
In 4th yr, thermodynamics, heat treatment of steels, and physical metallurgy parts like mechanical metallurgy and phase transformation in metals are taught.
The Physical metallurgy part, roughly speaking, studies the microstructures of materials and covers why mechanical properties are the way they are and how to control them.
In the Materials science part, you learn about polymers, composite materials, ceramics, and electronic materials.

INFO: Metallurgy Engineering

In 5th yr 1st Sem,
From the Extractive part,
For Pyrometallurgy, you learn how to solve problems using equations for processes like copper, iron, steel that require heating and melting.
Next, in mechanical Metallurgy, you learn the mechanical parts like hot rolling, cold rolling, which are used to shape iron to produce pipes and iron rods, and about welding.
In Nanotechnology, you learn about nanomaterials and their making processes.
In Characterization, you learn how to use tests to inspect materials, how to measure quality, and measure properties and composition.

In 5th yr 2nd sem, you study a subject called production management.
To study and find solutions for materials in bridges, ships, and cars rusting (corrosion) over time and cracking/failing, corrosion engineering and Fracture Mechanics and Failure Analysis are also taught.

Finally, you study hydro and electro Metallurgy which remain from the extractive part.
In these subjects, things like producing materials using chemical solutions processes,
and calculating the production of materials using Electrolysis are covered.
After all subjects, all fifth-year students must work as a team on a research project as a subject.

Since the results are already out, for the juniors who haven't considered or decided which university to attend, we want to share about our Yangon University.
When you say Yangon University, people usually first think of it as the university with the big Thitpok tree and the grand Convocation Hall.
Actually, Yangon University is a historic and iconic university in Myanmar that has stood for over a hundred years.
It is a university that has produced many outstanding and great people prominent in history.
Even now, it continues to produce outstanding (COE) students.
At our Yangon University, there are various majors.
You can choose and attend the major you like.
The main advantage of our university is that you absolutely do not spend money on tuition fees because there is no need to take tuitions.
Since there is no tuition, the university teachers do not teach lightly either.
For the children in their majors, they treat them exactly like their own children and teach and guide them in every aspect.
As for the teaching methodology, just as teachers explain the lessons, there are always group projects and presentations that you have to do yourself.
Besides, science major students have to do real hands-on practicals and take practical exams as well.
Furthermore, there are two libraries at the university: the Yangon University Library and the Universities' Central Library.
The Universities' Central Library is absolutely international in style.
Yangon University is also a university linked with many foreign scholars.
Moreover, just by registering as a Yangon University student, you can use free wifi within the university premises (Main, RC2, and hostels).
Yangon University is divided into two locations: Main and RC2.
First-year students study at RC2 (Hlaing Campus).

From the second year onwards, you study at the main campus.
However, first-year students taking science subjects have to come to the main campus to do practicals.
For students coming from far away towns, they can live in the hostels provided by the university.
First-year students have to stay in the hostels inside RC2.
Regarding the hostels, there is absolutely nothing to complain about.
You will stay two persons per room in air-conditioned rooms.
Electricity is available 24 hours.
In addition, welcomes and farewells are organized by each individual major, so there are many festivals and events.
There are also various kinds of sports clubs.
During the sports month in December, you can go and watch many sports events.
Because it is close to Hledan, where young people have many memories, you can often go and visit.
You can also visit recreational places like Inya Lake and Sein Lann So Pyay within a short time.

Since university admissions are coming out soon, I will share what I know about UFL for those interested.
UFL stands for University of Foreign languages.
There are only two such universities in the whole of Myanmar: YUFL and MUFL.
UFL universities are A class level universities and are especially prominent for their strict discipline.
Both YUFL and MUFL teach English, Chinese, Korean, Russia, German, France, Thai, and Japan languages with the same syllabus.
As a special feature, besides the languages mentioned above, they also have EBP major and EPC major.
EPC major (English for professional communication) is only available at YUFL, and EBP major (English for business purposes) is only available at MUFL.
In the first year for all majors, along with your chosen major, you have to study the following 3 minors: Linguistics, Aspect of Myanmar, Myanmar or Economic Geography (for EBP major).
You will receive a B.A Arts degree, and if you meet the qualifications, you can continue to attend for Master degrees.
As for admission marks, according to last year, people with 380 and above can apply.
The higher your marks, the more major choices you will have.
Because they are universities with the most festivals and events, you will fully experience uni life.
Regarding Job Opportunities, if you are a UFL graduate, there are many paths you can take.
Jobs related to Tourist guides, domestic and foreign companies related to your respective language, embassies, Language Tr, etc., there are plenty of opportunities.
If you are a UFL graduate, you will have a better standing than others in any workplace.
It is also a university with many scholarship connections.
For everyone, when choosing a university, if you want a practical degree that takes only four years to complete, I invite you to come to UFL universities.

I would like to briefly introduce the University of Medicine.
In Myanmar, there are a total of 6 medical universities, including 5 under the ministry and one under the NSA.
All 6 universities are under the Ministry of Health, and employment is guaranteed immediately upon graduation.
What makes medical school different from other health-related fields is the system used.
It is called the Integrated System.
This means that for the Musculoskeletal system, normal and abnormal conditions are taught together.
Therefore, when studying each system, there are anywhere from 6 major/minor subjects up to 12 subjects.
With this system, you cannot just cram right before the exam.
Since it takes 50% for Continuous Assessment and 50% for Final Assessment, it is easy to pass, but if you fail, you tend to fail the whole block/set.
In Continuous Assessment, there are Individual Assignments, Group discussion, Project, Group Presentation, Role Play, Speech Round, I rat, T rat, and Poster Presentation.
There are many classroom activities, so it can be tiring.
The duration of schooling is:
Introductory course 2months
Year 1 11 months
Year 2.
11 months
This period is called the Pre-clinical or Basic Medical Period.
In the Introductory course, Anatomy (Structural Principle), Physiology (Functional Principle), Biochemistry (Molecular Principle), Microbiology (Principle of Medical Microbiology), Pathology (Principle of Disease Mechanism), and Pharmacology (Principle of Drug Action) are taught fundamentally within 2 months.
This is a period where many students get weeded out.
The volume of material is immense.
In Year 1 and Year 2, the 10 systems of the body are divided and taught, and each system is continually taught with the above 6 subjects.

After Year 3, the duration of schooling is 11 months per year.
Year 3 is the clinical Junior year, and you start attending hospital wards.
You learn practically at various wards and attend lectures at school.
Years 4 and 5 are widely known as Final Part 1 and 2, which are the most important parts, and it is the period when Medical Trips begin.
Upon completing medical school, starting from those who passed in 2022, those who attend at state expense must sign a 5-year bond.
After that, they can sit the exam to transition from Assistant Surgeon to Specialist and work as a Lecturer, or stand as a Specialist.
Since records of your performance are kept year by year in medical school, you need to try very hard and carefully.
Introduction to UM
University of Medicine Introduction
-----------------------------------------
First of all, I would like to say Congratulations to all younger brothers and sisters.
Since the Grade 12 scores are out, I think you are considering which university to attend.
I will share information about medical school for younger brothers and sisters who are considering attending the University of Medicine.
Admission
----------
Admission to the University of Medicine must be applied through the university admission application form like other schools.
If you want to apply for the University of Medicine, you just need to write 'University of Medicine' on the application form.
(You do not need to add Yangon, Mandalay, Magway, Taunggyi, or Naypyidaw at the back.) The admission cutoff score changes from year to year.
In our year, they mainly looked at the combined score of the three ECB subjects.
They sort the scores from highest to lowest and cut off at their specified population limit.

In our year, they only called five hundred people, so the score went up quite high.
Do not feel discouraged looking at that huge score.
If you are passionate about it, just apply.
Getting in or not depends on luck.
Also, taking the medical entrance exam is just for calling people to meet.
An important point here is that the admission scores for the University of Medicine do not vary (higher or lower) depending on the school.
The admission cutoff score for all 6 medical schools is the same, and the school you attend is determined solely based on the region or state where you passed Grade 12.
For example, if you pass Grade 12 from Magway Region, you will get admission to the University of Medicine (Magway).
To guess which school you might get: in our year, some from Rakhine, Bago, Mahlaing exam centers and some townships in Yangon got Medicine 2; Lower Myanmar and the remaining townships of Yangon got Medicine 1; Kachin side and Mandalay side got Medicine Mandalay; some from Mahlaing exam center and everyone who passed from Magway got Medicine Magway; Shan got Medicine Taunggyi; and those from Naypyidaw fell into the NSA.
Sorry if I am wrong, since it's not my school, I don't know it very well.
Academic Year
---------------

If you attend the University of Medicine, you have to study for 6 academic years.
From 11/2025, you will have to take the Introductory course.
By early 2026, you will reach M1.
IC, M1, M2 are taught at school, while M3, M4, M5 are Clinical Years, so they are taught both at school and at the hospital.
In the M5 class, you will rotate and study in four departments at the hospital: Medicine, Surgery, Child, and OG.
Each class lasts for one year, and after completing M5, there will be a 1-year House Surgeon (internship) period.
Degree
-----
Upon completing the house surgeon period, you will receive an M.B.,B.S (Bachelor of Medicine, Bachelor of Surgery).
You can continue to attend postgraduate and doctoral degrees according to your own desire.
Subjects
-----------------
You can see the subjects to be taught year by year while attending the University of Medicine in the picture below.
Career Opportunities
------------------------------------------
There are many career opportunities.
When you finish school, you can enter government jobs, or you can work in NGOs and INGOs.
There are also people who work in private hospitals and those who go to work abroad.
If you want to continue postgraduate studies, you need to enter government service.
What kind of people should apply?
---------------------------------------------------
As for what kind of people should apply, I only want people who are genuinely interested and genuinely want to become doctors to apply.
I don't want you to apply just because your score meets the requirement, thinking lightly about wearing a duty coat and stethoscope, looking fancy and elegant.
This is because the University of Medicine takes longer compared to other schools.

Since it does not use the First Semester, Second Semester system like other universities, there are fewer holidays and many exams.
The gap between exams is at most about 2 months.
When you reach higher classes, it's about once a month.
Furthermore, since the work you will do after graduation involves human lives, I want you to apply only after thinking deeply.
Otherwise, if you drop out of school while attending because you lose interest or cannot endure the exhaustion, or if you keep failing exams, it's a waste of time.
You also feel apologetic to your parents.
It's not good for you either, so I want you to think very carefully.
Once you have applied and are about to attend, it becomes your responsibility to complete school, get the degree, and have confidence when treating patients, so you need to fulfill your duty.
Not everyone who attends medical school becomes a doctor.
That said, the University of Medicine can give you a full university student life experience.
There are fun things depending on the class and the school.
For those who want to become doctors, the feeling of wearing a Duty Coat and the feeling of learning while communicating and interacting with patients are truly sweet and enjoyable.
However, you also need to think deeply and try hard with a serious mind and perseverance.
What should be prepared?
-------------------------------------------
As for what should be prepared, the main thing is to maintain the momentum from Grade 12 without breaking it.
Although there are many exams, you get used to it over time.
However, you cannot live lightly at the University of Medicine thinking 'Ah, I have reached university!'.
The exam system is a Compartment System, so just passing the Final Exam is not enough.

You will only pass if you get the required marks in the respective Tests.
Saying that doesn't mean you have to study and only study.
There is also enough time to create fun memories of university student life.
The main thing is to have fun when it's time to have fun, attend class when it's time to attend class, and study when it's time to study, doing it systematically according to its time; then I guarantee you will not regret attending medical school.
Since being a doctor is a life-long learning process, you will have to read books until old age.
But since a doctor's life is noble enough to be proud of, I invite younger brothers and sisters who are genuinely interested and those who do not know for sure yet to make a thoughtful decision and come along, I welcome you.

You will also have to study other business-related minor and major subjects.

THM (Tourism and Hospitality Management)

Journalism (Journalism)

English for Professional purposes
• What is B.A(EPP)?
B.A (EPP) is an undergraduate course opened jointly by the "National Management Degree College" and the "Yangon University English Department".
The long form is Bachelor of Arts (English for Professional Purposes).
In Myanmar, it can be described as "Workplace English, Vocational English".
EPP is a business management + business English major built on the backbone of Business Management.
The training period is (4) years, and you will receive a B.A (EPP) degree accredited by Yangon University.
• What is taught in B.A (EPP)?
Subjects taught in EPP can be divided into (2) parts: Workplace English and Business Management.
Workplace English teaches the English proficiency required to write and speak effectively in workplaces where English is spoken.
Business Management is for performing business management.
Subjects taught regarding Workplace English are:
• English for Business
• Communicative Skills
• English Pronunciation for Clear Communication
• English in the Media
• Presentation and Public Speaking Skills
• English for Meetings
• English for Negotiation
• Translation and Interpretation
• Introduction to Pragmatics
• Research Paper Writing.

History of YUON

🔶 I will also tell you a little bit about the history of the University of Nursing (Yangon).

The University of Nursing (Yangon) is located at No. (677/709), Bogyoke Aung San Road, Lanmadaw Township. It spans an area of 6.971 acres.

🔸 The construction of the University of Nursing (Yangon) began in March 1984 and was completed in February 1986 as a symbol of friendship and cooperation between Japan and the Pyidaungsu Socialist Thammada Myanma Naingngandaw (Wohh, it has been 36 years already!).

Initially, in July 1986, it was founded under the name Nurses Training Center (Yangon). (3)-year Nursing diploma courses were initially taught.

On November 5, 1991, the name was changed to Institute of Nursing (Yangon) (ION), and the status was upgraded to a university level. It became the first university to confer a Bachelor of Nursing Science degree in Myanmar. It started teaching in-service nurses who already passed the nursing diploma to become degree holders with BNSc (bridge) courses.

🔸 In 1994, the BNSc (Generic) course, which requires full-time attendance for (4) years, was successfully launched, teaching those who passed the university entrance exam and met the annual admission criteria.

Due to the increasing number of students studying at the University of Nursing, in March 1996, an extension new building was built on a plot of 8.644 acres between Bogyoke Aung San Road and Min Ye Kyaw Swa Road across the street, and it was opened on June 5, 1997.

In 2005, the University of Nursing was upgraded to the University of Nursing (Yangon) YUON.

1. BNSc (Generic) 4 years course
2. BNSc (Bridge) 1 year (on-campus) course

In postgraduate courses:
1. Diploma in Emergency Nursing (PG Diploma, 15 months)
2. Master of Nursing Science (MNSc) (Specialty) (Mental Health Nursing, Nursing Administration and Management, Adult Health Nursing, Critical Care Nursing, Maternal Health Nursing, Child Health Nursing, Community Health Nursing) courses, as well as
3. PhD (Nursing) courses have been successfully opened and taught.

Note: MNSc (General Nursing) and MNSc (Orthopedic Nursing) courses are not included in 2023.

INFO: You have to attend 4/5 months per semester.
After the First Semester, you get a holiday of about 16-18 days.
Then you have to attend the next semester.
After the Second Semester,
it closes for about 1 month to 1 and a half months before attending the next year.
The First Year is divided into 3 semesters.
Holidays are quite few.

INFO: At the training school, you only attend for 3 years and get a Diploma, dear.
At the university, you have to attend for 4 years and get a Bachelor's degree.

INFO: There is no such thing as an untiring university or profession.
It differs based on your acceptance of tiredness and how you live to stay relaxed.
On exam days, you just have to study; for the remaining days, reading and studying depend only on the individual.
Although it is tiring, it is not like that every day.
You are a student, so you will have to pass through assignments/exams related to students.
Looking at it from the perspective of nursing, nursing will be tiring.
Other universities are also the same; they have stress regarding the minor and major subjects they study.
For me, I have fun when it's time to have fun.
I do my work when it's time to work.
Just sitting and shouting that you are frustrated or tired won't make you pass the exam.
On holidays, go outside and enjoy when there's fun.
If you follow your own routine, attending university is very fun.
The study material is a lot.
Some people post it just to get attention.
Relax, okay, babies? 🫰🏻

Official 2025 admission extract: Applicants must pass the 2025 Matriculation Exam and apply directly to NMDC in Botahtaung Township when separate newspaper advertisements are published. Programs are all 4 years: Bachelor of Business Management (BBM), intake 200; Bachelor of Tourism and Hospitality Management (BTHM), intake 200; Bachelor of Arts in English for Professional Purposes (B.A. EPP), intake 120; Bachelor of Arts in Journalism, intake 120; Bachelor of Economics and Finance (BEF), intake 200.

Official 2025 admission extract for the University of Dental Medicine in Yangon and Mandalay: accepted students are 60% male and 40% female. Applicants must pass the 2025 Matriculation Exam with the STEAMS-1 combination of Chemistry, Physics, and Biology, have a 6-subject total score of 450 or above, and hold a National Scrutiny Card (N). Preliminary selection uses the combined marks of English, Chemistry, and Biology. Shortlisted candidates must pass an interview/oral examination and a psychological assessment. The top 200 students are selected. Students must sign a bond to serve as a civil servant for at least 5 years, plus another 5 years if pursuing postgraduate studies. Breach of bond requires 70,000,000 kyats compensation and the Dental License will not be issued. Up to 30% of accepted students may study at their own expense by paying separate tuition if they refuse civil service.

Official 2025 admission extract: YUFL applicants must pass the 2025 Matriculation Exam, may apply from anywhere in Myanmar, and must hold a National Scrutiny Card (N). For the English major, applicants need at least 60 marks in English. For English for Professional Communication, applicants need at least 65 marks in English. Programs are 4-year B.A. degrees in Chinese, English, French, German, Japanese, Korean, Russian, Thai, and English for Professional Communication.

Official 2025 admission extract for University of Medicine 1, Yangon: accepted students are 50% male and 50% female. Applicants must pass the 2025 Matriculation Exam with STEAMS-1 (Chemistry, Physics, Biology), have a 6-subject total score of 450 or above, and hold a National Scrutiny Card (N). Preliminary selection is based on English, Chemistry, and Biology marks. Shortlisted candidates must pass an interview/oral examination and a psychological assessment. The top 1,250 students across all medical universities are selected. Students must sign a bond to serve as a civil servant for at least 5 years, plus 5 more years for postgraduate study. Breach of bond has a 60,000,000 kyats penalty and the General Medical License (Sama) will be withheld. Up to 30% may attend at their own expense by paying separate tuition if they opt out of civil service.

Official 2025 admission extract: YUECO applicants must pass the 2025 Matriculation Exam with any subject combination and hold a National Scrutiny Card (N). Hlaing Campus is open to students nationwide. Ywarthagyi Campus is restricted to students from Kayin, Tanintharyi, Bago, Mon, Rakhine, Yangon, and Ayeyarwady regions. Programs are all 4 years: Bachelor of Economics (Economics), Bachelor of Public Administration (BPA), Bachelor of Development Studies (B.DevS), Bachelor of Economics (Statistics), Bachelor of Population Studies (BPS), Bachelor of Commerce (B.Com), Bachelor of Accounting (B.Act), Bachelor of Business Administration (BBA), and Bachelor of Actuarial Science (BAS).

Official 2025 admission extract for the University of Pharmacy in Yangon and Mandalay: there are no specific male/female percentage limits. Applicants must pass the 2025 Matriculation Exam with STEAMS-1 (Chemistry, Physics, Biology) and hold a National Scrutiny Card (N). Students must sign a bond to serve as a civil servant for at least 5 years, plus 5 years for postgraduate studies. Breach of bond requires 25,000,000 kyats compensation. Up to 20% of the 350 accepted students can study at their own expense by paying separate tuition fees.

Official 2025 admission extract for the University of Veterinary Science, Yezin: accepted students are 50% male and 50% female. Applicants must pass the 2025 Matriculation Exam with STEAMS-1, be citizens, and apply directly to the university when advertised in newspapers using A4 paper. Doctor of Veterinary Medicine (DVM) is 5 years, requires a total matriculation score of 410 or above, and has intake 75 males and 75 females, 150 total. Bachelor of Animal Science (BASc) is 4 years, requires a total score of 350 or above, and has intake 75 males and 75 females, 150 total. Students receiving government stipends or scholarships must serve as civil servants for 5 years after graduation.

Official 2025 admission extract for the University of Community Health, Magway: accepted students are 80% male and 20% female. Applicants must pass the 2025 Matriculation Exam with STEAMS-1 and hold a National Scrutiny Card (N). Students must sign a bond to serve as a civil servant for at least 5 years, plus 5 years for postgraduate studies. Breach of bond requires 25,000,000 kyats compensation.

Official 2025 admission extract for Myanmar Maritime University: applicants and parents must hold a National Scrutiny Card (N). Maximum female intake is 20%. Total matriculation score must be 420 or above. Applicants must pass STEAMS-1 or STEAMS-2, undergo a medical checkup with Health Class A, pass a color blindness test, attend an interview, and apply directly when advertised. Females cannot apply for Marine Engineering, Marine Electrical Systems and Electronics, or Nautical Science. Nautical Science applicants must not wear glasses. Programs are 5 years, 360 students total: B.E. Naval Architecture 40, B.E. Marine Engineering 60, B.E. Port and Harbour Engineering 40, B.E. River and Coastal Engineering 40, B.E. Marine Electrical Systems and Electronics Engineering 60, B.E. Marine Mechanical Engineering 40, and B.Sc. (Honours) Nautical Science 80.

Official 2025 admission extract for Yangon Technological University: accepted students are 55% male and 45% female, with total intake 400. Applicants must pass the 2025 Matriculation Exam with at least 300 combined marks in Physics, Chemistry, Math, and English. Any subject combination containing Math, Chemistry, and Physics is allowed. Applicants must hold a National Scrutiny Card (N) and be fit for practical/factory fieldwork. Selection is based on the highest combined score of Physics, Chemistry, Math, and English. Programs are 5-year B.E. or B.Arch: Civil 50, Mechanical 50, Electrical Power 50, Electronics 35, Telecommunication 30, Computer Engineering and IT 35, Mechatronics 35, Chemical 30, Food 20, Textile 20, Mining 15, Petroleum 15, Metallurgy and Materials Science 20, and Architecture 30.

Official 2025 admission extract for West Yangon Technological University: WYTU is categorized under Technological Universities. Accepted students are 60% male and 40% female. Applicants must pass the 2025 Matriculation Exam with STEAMS-1 or STEAMS-2, have at least 240 combined marks in English, Math, Physics, and Chemistry, and hold a National Scrutiny Card (N). Priority is given to local region students. Programs include B.E. and B.Arch degrees with 5-year duration and B.S. Bio-Tech with 4-year duration.

Official 2025 admission extract for Yangon University of Education: accepted students are 50% male and 50% female. Applicants must pass the 2025 Matriculation Exam with STEAMS-1, STEAMS-2, STAMS-1, or STAMS-2. It is open to students from Kayin, Tanintharyi, Bago, Mon, Rakhine, Yangon, and Ayeyarwady regions.
